text.title: Testing in Go Procedure
text.summary: 6-step procedure to apply testing-go. Each step names its input, action, and output. Steps may not be reordered without explicit deviation note.

step.name: Enumerate the public API of the package under test.
step.description: Input: package source. Action: List every exported func/method + its input/output types.. Output: function inventory.

step.name: For each function, choose a test shape: pure-logic → table-driven; HTTP handler → `httptest.NewRecorder`; client → `httptest.NewServer`; performance-critical → add a `Benchmark*`.
step.description: Input: function inventory. Action: Match each function to a test shape.. Output: test plan table.

step.name: Define the table struct with named fields per case (`name`, inputs, expected outputs, expected error).
step.description: Input: test plan table. Action: Author table; cover zero/boundary/error cases.. Output: table literal.
step.note: Step 3. Required input: test plan table.

step.name: Write the `t.Run` loop with `tc := tc` (Go <1.22) and `t.Parallel()` where safe; use `require` for nil/error preconditions.
step.description: Input: table literal. Action: Compile and run.. Output: passing test file.

step.name: For benchmarks: write `Benchmark*` using `b.Loop()`; for fuzz: write `Fuzz*` with seed corpus.
step.description: Input: performance/security-sensitive functions. Action: Add to same `*_test.go`.. Output: bench + fuzz funcs.

step.name: Gate slow tests behind `//go:build integration`; document `go test -tags=integration ./...` in README.
step.description: Input: slow tests. Action: Add build tag header and CI step.. Output: integration tag.
